results.title: Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial - PubMed
results.content:
Mitochondrial dysfunction and stem cell exhaustion contribute to age-related immune decline, yet clinical interventions targeting immune aging are lacking. Recently, we demonstrated that urolithin A (UA), a mitophagy inducer, expands T memory stem cells (TSCM) and naive T cells in mice. In this randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial, 50 healthy middle-aged adults received oral UA (1,000 mg day-1) or placebo for 4 weeks; time points of analysis were baseline and day 28. Primary outcomes were phenotypical changes in peripheral CD3+ T cell subsets and immune metabolic remodeling. results.title: Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial - PMC
results.content:
Mitochondrial dysfunction and stem cell exhaustion contribute to age-related immune decline, yet clinical interventions targeting immune aging are lacking. Recently, we demonstrated that urolithin A (UA), a mitophagy inducer, expands T memory stem cells (TSCM) and naive T cells in mice. In this randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial, 50 healthy middle-aged adults received oral UA (1,000 mg day−1) or placebo for 4 weeks; time points of analysis were baseline and day 28. Primary outcomes were phenotypical changes in peripheral CD3+ T cell subsets and immune metabolic remodeling. UA expanded peripheral naive-like, less terminally exhausted CD8+ cells (treatment difference 0.50 percentage points; 95% CI = 0.16 to 0.83; P = 0.0437) while also increasing CD8+ fatty acid oxidation [...] across immune populations, modulating pathways linked to inflammation and metabolism. These findings indicate that short-term UA supplementation modulates human immune cell composition and function, supporting its potential to counteract age-related immune decline and inflammaging. ClinicalTrials.gov registration number: NCT05735886. [...] Collectively, our data provide strong evidence that altering mitochondrial dynamics via the orally available and well-tolerated mitophagy inducer UA elicits systemic immune remodeling and potentially reduces the immune aging phenotype. We therefore provide an intervention-based approach to limiting immune aging that may have potential to benefit chronic conditions characterized by immune dysfunction.

results.title: Author Correction: Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial - PMC
results.content:
PMC Copyright notice

PMCID: PMC12920124  PMID: 41491872

This corrects the article "Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial" in volume 5 on page 2309.

Correction to: Nature Aging 10.1038/s43587-025-00996-x, published online 31 October 2025.

In the version of the article initially published, Fig. 3e was inadvertently a duplicate of Fig. 3b. Fig. 3 has now been amended with the correct panel e in the HTML and PDF versions of the article.
